journal_entry.description: Settlement of accounts payable by cheque
journal_entry.rationale: Payment of existing liability (accounts payable) reduces the liability account (debit) and reduces cash asset (credit). The transaction is straightforward: settling AP with cash. No tax applies as this is settlement of a pre-existing liability where tax would have been recorded at the time of the original purchase. Debit tuple (0,0,0,1,0,0) indicates 1 liability decrease line. Credit tuple (0,0,0,1,0,0) indicates 1 asset decrease line.

output.reason: Settling accounts payable means paying off an existing liability. This decreases the liability (accounts payable), which is recorded as a debit. One liability decrease line.

debit_classifier.reason: Settling accounts payable means paying off an existing liability. This decreases the liability (accounts payable), which is recorded as a debit. One liability decrease line.

output.reason: Issuing a cheque means cash is leaving the business. Cash is an asset, so when it decreases, it is credited. This transaction has one credit line: asset decrease (cash).

credit_classifier.reason: Issuing a cheque means cash is leaving the business. Cash is an asset, so when it decreases, it is credited. This transaction has one credit line: asset decrease (cash).
